name: Buffered Surfaces & Post-Processing

userStory.iWant: to render components to offscreen surfaces
userStory.soThat: I can layer them with z-index, cache expensive renders, and apply post-processing effects.

acceptanceCriteria: Surface type represents a 2D grid of Cell objects
Cell object contains char, hex, bg, and modifiers
offscreen() captures a render function's output into a Surface
blt() performs a block transfer of one surface onto another with clipping
opacity() correctly blends a source cell's color with a target cell's background
filter() applies a fragment shader function (Cell -> Cell) to every cell in a surface

testPlan.goldenPath: Render a complex component (Table) to a surface, blit it to a main surface at (10,5), and assert the cell contents at specific coordinates match the original output.
edgeCases: Blitting a source surface entirely outside the target bounds
Partial clipping on all four edges
testPlan.fuzzStress: Allocate 1000x1000 surfaces and perform 100 sequential blit operations to monitor memory footprint and latency.
failureCases: Incompatible cell dimensions during blend operations
OOM errors on massive buffer allocations

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(W * H) for rendering and composition
estimatedComplexity.memoryFootprint: Width * Height * sizeof(Cell) bytes per surface
